channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA changes will not be introduced this spring after all and [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to 100 Mile House oncology department, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural Cariboo communities build lasting digital storytelling capacity that preserves cultural memory while maintaining sovereignty over their own narratives? Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA cha...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada’s alcohol relief ‘step in the...
  • CBC: [CBC] Williams Lake, Quesnel and 100 Mile House firefighters...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New power for B.C. city’s mayor to r...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Regional district, B.C in stalemate ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Vancouver Island WildFire Whisky pai...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prescribed burns set for north ea...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Benefits of home-ground flour shared a...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Some of the buzziest names in superintell...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Turn shoebox memories into digital keepsakes w...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Surge in demand overwhelms West Koot...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Okanagan Humane Society: An affectio...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. police watchdog probes Quesnel ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Hackaday Podcast Episode 366: DOOM on a Toaster, ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural Cariboo communities build lasting digital storytelling capacity that preserves cultural memory while maintaining sovereignty over their own narratives? Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceAlert] Tiny Microbes Hiding in Soil May Help Pull Rain From The Sky, Study Reveals and [New Atlas] This all-female fish species reproduces without sex by cloning itself, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities develop the interpretive capacity to convert scientific findings into local ecological and economic decisions? Topics include: The gap between scientific knowledge production and local implementation in rural regions, Integration of Indigenous/Traditional Ecological Knowledge with Western science while preserving integrity, Role of extension services and knowledge broker models in translating research, Cariboo rural resilience mechanisms and economic diversity evolution, Community interpretive capacity as prerequisite to ecological governance and decision-making authority.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Tiny Microbes Hiding in Soil May Help Pull Ra...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] This all-female fish species reproduces without ...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] This phone actually has a built-in walkie-talki...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Now we're cooking with water! Startup offers a h...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Train derailment north of Lytton
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake youth bowler returns f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake teenager running for B...

Additional sources provided:

  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Do Or Die: How EV Startups Rivian, Lucid, And Sl...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'A mosaic-like pattern': This new ‘Swiss-Army’ o...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] ARCHIVES: Centennial Park bike park pl...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Using Capacitance for Extremely Sensitive Proximi...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I set up my Obsidian vault to organize itse...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Update immediately': 60,000 WordPress websites ...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Review: UGREEN NASync DXP4800 Pro refresh with new ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'No onboard pilot needed': Massive 2-ton VTOL “m...
  • Newsletter: r***@substack.com: Handheld duster/GetYourGuide.com/Expert photo tip
  • Newsletter: r***@substack.com: Handheld duster/GetYourGuide.com/Expert photo tip
  • Newsletter: r***@substack.com: Handheld duster/GetYourGuide.com/Expert photo tip

Closing Music: “Basorexia” by JARA — pop, rocknroll, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile fire crews douse blaze that spread between yards on Kennedy Road and [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: Another storage shed claimed by fire at Mountain Spruce Community Centre, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do small rural communities like those in the Cariboo actually make public decisions, and are the formal mechanisms available to them functioning effectively? Topics include: Williams Lake cannabis licensing decision as a case study in local democratic procedure versus substance, Canada Post service changes as an example of decisions made remotely without meaningful local input, Civic technology tools (meeting transcription, decision dashboards, participatory budgeting) and their applicability to small municipalities, The gap between consultation and genuine decision-making power in rural governance, Indigenous governance models (Tŝilhqot'in Nation) as contrasts to current non-Indigenous local democratic processes.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Hospice Society

Sources discussed:

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile fire crews douse blaze that s...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: Another storage shed claimed ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Tk’emlúps police medic overcomes rug...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Rainforest to Gold Rush train trip t...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Switch to community mailboxes expect...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Police, scientists to ‘track and tra...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. report finds gaps remain in det...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] New ‘mosaic’ of national and provincial parks ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Japan’s Cherry Blossom Database, 1,200 Yea...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I spent a month with Cursor, Google Antigra...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Mice that ate artificial sweeteners passed met...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] I tested Jackery's super-compact portable power ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Application for new cannabis store i...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Quesnel woman wins $76K in Lotto 6/4...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Vehicle fire extinguished Friday mor...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Northern Health launches survey to i...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Large avalanches heighten continued ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Haz-mat emergency saw several young ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Multiple injuries after vehicle slam...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Fixing a GameCube’s Dodgy Optical Drive With Fres...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Automated filtration system promises drinking wa...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Your old iPad or Android tablet can be your new smar...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I used this utility to gain complete contro...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Essentially no human intervention': Chinese AI ...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] This open-source smart home system is slowly o...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] UGreen NASync iDX6011 Pro NAS review: An AI-powe...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Misophonia Has Genetic Links to Anxiety And D...

Closing Music: “Behind The Walls” by Non Camera — pop, rock, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] Springs events ramping up in Lac La Hache and [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Surreal’: Logan Lake resident wins $1M in lottery, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can technology actually protect communities during acute emergencies (floods, fires) when the gap between notification systems and genuine preparedness is fundamentally social and structural, not technical? Topics include: Gaps between notification systems (AlertBC) and genuine community preparedness in the acute emergency moment, How community-built emergency management systems outperform vendor-imposed platforms in rural and Indigenous contexts, Nature-based flood solutions (wetland restoration, floodplain reconnection) enabled by LiDAR and remote sensing technology, and their political economy implications, Indigenous territorial governance as enabling long-term ecological planning and data stewardship across generations, Data sovereignty and institutional continuity in environmental monitoring and emergency management.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Scout Island Nature Centre

Sources discussed:

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Springs events ramping up in Lac La Ha...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Surreal’: Logan Lake resident wins ...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I reclaimed 15 hours this week with AI Agents —...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Scientists Finally Measured The Mind-Blowing ...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Much-Hyped Alzheimer's Drugs Show No Meaningf...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] EDITOR: Local ER closures are unaccept...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Norovirus outbreak at complex car...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] An oil and gas company just left behind an est...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Malfunctioning Canadian LNG terminal burned mo...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] Anthropic just released Opus 4.7 — the 'civilia...

Additional sources provided:

  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Was the Grand Canyon Born from an Ancient Lake Sp...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Strengthening emergency support for evacuees
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] The Cockroach of Dinosaurs Likely Survived Extinct...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] How Companies Hijack AI Chatbots
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Track sharing agreement on horizon f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Vaisakhi unites Williams Lake commun...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Comparing 12 VDC Air Fryers with Regular 240 VAC ...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] These companies are actually upskilling their worker...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I recommended this SBC before the Raspberry...
  • CNET: [CNET] Swiss Privacy Goes Global: Proton VPN Grows Coverage ...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Scientists Reveal Why Bread Can Cause Weight ...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Single-vehicle crash in Quesnel l...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Can Nature Help Solve the Flood Threat Humans Cre...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Encouraging people to prepare for seasonal haz...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] Researchers have found that some aspects of sp...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can technology actually protect communities during acute emergencies (floods, fires) when the gap between notification systems and genuine preparedness is fundamentally social and structural, not technical? Topics include: Gaps between notification systems (AlertBC) and genuine community preparedness in the acute emergency moment, How community-built emergency management systems outperform vendor-imposed platforms in rural and Indigenous contexts, Nature-based flood solutions (wetland restoration, floodplain reconnection) enabled by LiDAR and remote sensing technology, and their political economy implications, Indigenous territorial governance as enabling long-term ecological planning and data stewardship across generations, Data sovereignty and institutional continuity in environmental monitoring and emergency management.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Preventing AI extractivism: the case for braiding indigenous data justice with ABS for stronger AI data governance and TMU students to safeguard Residential School Survivor testimonies, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What infrastructure, governance, and safeguards must be in place before Indigenous communities build digital archives to ensure long-term functionality, community control, and protection from data extraction? Topics include: Format obsolescence and technical sovereignty in digital preservation, Shared infrastructure governance models versus isolated community systems, AI extractivism and machine-readable metadata protection (TK Labels), Temporal asymmetry between knowledge preservation and data extraction, Regional Indigenous consortium potential for shared technical capacity in the Cariboo.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Preventing AI extractivism: the case for braiding indigenous...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : TMU students to safeguard Residential School Survivor testim...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Five headlines from a major NWT health privacy report
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : “Records have a spirit and need to be treated with respect...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : “Records have a spirit and need to be treated with respect...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : “Records have a spirit and need to be treated with respect...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Professor Krystal Tsosie Advocates for Indigenous-Led Scienc...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Nakota communities reclaim audio recordings housed at Indian...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. introduces legislation to ratif...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Prescribed burn planned at Horsefly ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] BREAKING: RCMP responding to reports...

Additional sources provided:

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Preventing AI extractivism: the case for braiding indigenous data justice with ABS for stronger AI data governance and TMU students to safeguard Residential School Survivor testimonies, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What infrastructure, governance, and safeguards must be in place before Indigenous communities build digital archives to ensure long-term functionality, community control, and protection from data extraction? Topics include: Format obsolescence and technical sovereignty in digital preservation, Shared infrastructure governance models versus isolated community systems, AI extractivism and machine-readable metadata protection (TK Labels), Temporal asymmetry between knowledge preservation and data extraction, Regional Indigenous consortium potential for shared technical capacity in the Cariboo.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Preventing AI extractivism: the case for braiding indigenous...

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Immersed in Community (and Archives!) and Immersed in Community (and Archives!), plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for communities—particularly Indigenous nations in BC—to govern technology itself, rather than simply using it, and what institutional capacity is required to sustain that governance? Topics include: Distinction between using technology for governance versus governing technology itself, Design sustainability: the tension between data sovereignty restrictions and emergency technical support access, Archival governance and the asymmetric stakes of data loss for Indigenous nations versus municipalities, Funding models: grant dependency versus revenue-generating data governance and education-based capacity pipelines, The relationship between overall institutional health and digital governance capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Immersed in Community (and Archives!)
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Immersed in Community (and Archives!)
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] I upgraded to fiber and my Wi-Fi still lagged:...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. Conservatives make opening bid ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Mustangs close out 202...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] FCC just handed Netgear a de facto router monopol...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] BCGEU files unfair labour practic...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 100 Mile House rally draws over 150,...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Wave or Particle? One of the biggest ever mysteries...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] On the network effect of the weekend : “T...

Additional sources provided:

  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Niiwin Demo
  • CNET: [CNET] I Used to Tell People to Skip Wi-Fi 7 Routers. CNET's...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] K’ómoks Treaty Act introduced in B.C. legislat...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] How Joseph Trutch Set the Stage for BC’s Indigeno...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Driver speeds away and hits tree aft...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Original PlayStation Brought Up To Date
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] Google just unlocked 'Agent Mode' for Gemini 3....
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] War Exposes Gaps in India’s Clean Energy Push
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The Optocam Zero is a Raspberry Pi Zero cam...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Scientists May Now Know Why GLP-1s Don't Work...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] What if Dark Matter Is Actually Black Holes F...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A $40 label maker and QR codes completely c...
  • Newsletter: "Emma Gilchrist, The Narwhal" : The Narwhal | News on Climate Change, Environmental Issues i...
  • Newsletter: "Emma Gilchrist, The Narwhal" : Let’s get to know each other better
  • Newsletter: "Emma Gilchrist, The Narwhal" : Take 3 minutes to let us know more about you...

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for communities—particularly Indigenous nations in BC—to govern technology itself, rather than simply using it, and what institutional capacity is required to sustain that governance? Topics include: Distinction between using technology for governance versus governing technology itself, Design sustainability: the tension between data sovereignty restrictions and emergency technical support access, Archival governance and the asymmetric stakes of data loss for Indigenous nations versus municipalities, Funding models: grant dependency versus revenue-generating data governance and education-based capacity pipelines, The relationship between overall institutional health and digital governance capacity.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Cattle Were Poisoned by BC’s Forest Fertilizer. Now Someone Will Pay and [BC Gov News] Aerial spraying will curb invasive spongy moths in Delta, Squamish, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can mining governance and technology models be redesigned to ensure companies bear long-term liability for environmental damage rather than leaving taxpayers and communities holding the reclamation bill? Topics include: Bond calculation and reclamation cost estimation failures in BC mining history, Environmental monitoring technology and real-time data access as accountability tools, Indigenous co-governance arrangements and decision authority in mining oversight, Post-closure liability and orphan site funding models, Community technical capacity gaps during environmental assessment processes.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Cattle Were Poisoned by BC’s Forest Fertilizer. N...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Aerial spraying will curb invasive spongy moth...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] "Spin-flip" system pushes solar cell energy conv...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Ancient Landscape Reveals Bones of Humans Who...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Slate raises fresh $650 million as reserv...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Fire Department exting...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Mount Timothy ends winter season with ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile Fire Rescue celebrating 70th ...

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This ESP32 Macintosh Plus emulator can pull...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] A New Computer Chip Could Finally Withstand T...

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can mining governance and technology models be redesigned to ensure companies bear long-term liability for environmental damage rather than leaving taxpayers and communities holding the reclamation bill? Topics include: Bond calculation and reclamation cost estimation failures in BC mining history, Environmental monitoring technology and real-time data access as accountability tools, Indigenous co-governance arrangements and decision authority in mining oversight, Post-closure liability and orphan site funding models, Community technical capacity gaps during environmental assessment processes.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down #1334 The Long Fight for a Little Justice in Burns Lake and #172 Why Governments Want to Ban Kids From AI and Social Media, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What foundational conditions must exist in rural communities before digital storytelling infrastructure can meaningfully address the underrepresentation of their narratives? Topics include: The gap between cultural production capacity and platform/distribution infrastructure, Distinguishing between grant-dependent tools versus sustainable internal capacity-building, Indigenous communities' approach to language documentation as self-sustaining storytelling practice, Existing Cariboo cultural institutions as underutilized infrastructure nodes, Long-term custodianship and archival accessibility as core design principle.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • Newsletter: CANADALAND : #1334 The Long Fight for a Little Justice in Burns Lake
  • Newsletter: CANADALAND : #172 Why Governments Want to Ban Kids From AI and Social Med...

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] '71% of US households get routers from ISPs': Wh...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Does Burning Wood Actually Fight Climate Change?
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a $100 ePaper photo frame that neve...
  • Newsletter: CANADALAND : #1342 Nardwuar: An Oral History

  • Newsletter: OpenMedia : Big things are happening in March❗

  • Newsletter: CANADALAND : #1330 My Chatbot Encouraged my Psychosis

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down #1334 The Long Fight for a Little Justice in Burns Lake and #172 Why Governments Want to Ban Kids From AI and Social Media, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What foundational conditions must exist in rural communities before digital storytelling infrastructure can meaningfully address the underrepresentation of their narratives? item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers, students plant 500 trees to rejuvenate B.C. park after 2023 wildfire and [XDA Developers] The Linux kernel now allows AI-written code, but you're on the hook for it, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities transition from being passive data sources for external scientific projects to becoming active generators and owners of ecological research questions and findings? Topics include: Data sovereignty and the OCAP principles in community science partnerships, Knowledge extraction versus epistemic collaboration with Indigenous communities, Sustainable funding and governance models for long-term ecological monitoring, Role of technology (drones, sensors) in making community-scale ecological research economically viable, Community Futures organizations as potential intermediaries for ecological enterprise and stewardship compensation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers, students plant 500 trees...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The Linux kernel now allows AI-written code...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] An AI built a boutique with $100,000, the...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] '$15K bill destroyed a solo developer’s startup'...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Experimental Drug Can Reverse Osteoarthritis ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quartz: [Quartz] Can DUK's Grid Modernization Drive Long-Term Growth...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Testing Refrigerants and Capillary Tubes to Find ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A used Mac Mini could be great for your hom...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Layer height was sabotaging my 3D prints, a...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] A Suction-Driven Seven-Segment Display
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Implementing PCIe over Fiber Using SFP Modules
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Authenticate SSH with Your TPM
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] EXCLUSIVE: Eby discusses DRIPA, heal...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] ORICO X50 Thunderbolt 5 Portable SSD review: Blazin...
  • CNET: [CNET] Watch a Robot Stuff Cash Into a Wallet Just Like You ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] AI drones make mine clearing faster and safer

Closing Music: “Même” by Paul & Manuel — indie, chansonfrancaise, world. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-opus-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers, students plant 500 trees to rejuvenate B.C. park after 2023 wildfire and [XDA Developers] The Linux kernel now allows AI-written code, but you're on the hook for it, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities transition from being passive data sources for external scientific projects to becoming active generators and owners of ecological research questions and findings? Topics include: Data sovereignty and the OCAP principles in community science partnerships, Knowledge extraction versus epistemic collaboration with Indigenous communities, Sustainable funding and governance models for long-term ecological monitoring, Role of technology (drones, sensors) in making community-scale ecological research economically viable, Community Futures organizations as potential intermediaries for ecological enterprise and stewardship compensation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers, students plant 500 trees...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The Linux kernel now allows AI-written code...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] An AI built a boutique with $100,000, the...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] '$15K bill destroyed a solo developer’s startup'...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Experimental Drug Can Reverse Osteoarthritis ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quartz: [Quartz] Can DUK's Grid Modernization Drive Long-Term Growth...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Testing Refrigerants and Capillary Tubes to Find ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A used Mac Mini could be great for your hom...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Layer height was sabotaging my 3D prints, a...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] A Suction-Driven Seven-Segment Display
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Implementing PCIe over Fiber Using SFP Modules
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Authenticate SSH with Your TPM
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] EXCLUSIVE: Eby discusses DRIPA, heal...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] ORICO X50 Thunderbolt 5 Portable SSD review: Blazin...
  • CNET: [CNET] Watch a Robot Stuff Cash Into a Wallet Just Like You ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] AI drones make mine clearing faster and safer

Closing Music: “Même” by Paul & Manuel — indie, chansonfrancaise, world. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-opus-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. unemployment rate spikes 0.6 percentage points to 6.7 per cent in March and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. government easing carbon tax for pulp mills as industry grapples with future, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What constitutes community resilience infrastructure in rural Cariboo communities, and who actually maintains it? Topics include: Fire response infrastructure and volunteer emergency services, Air quality monitoring and vulnerable population access to health information, Cultural institutions as psychological resilience resources, Community foundation endowment models for sustaining local infrastructure, Indigenous Guardian programs as models for sustained locally-rooted capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. unemployment rate spikes 0.6...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. government easing carbon tax fo...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘The problem is Premier Eby’: B.C. N...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Judge scorches Okanagan Indian Band’...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Chairlift outage at B.C. ski resort ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. First Nation ‘confident’ in Isl...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Partnerships bring more maternity ca...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Implementing a Rhythm Game entirely in a GPU Shad...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] CPU-Z and HWMonitor downloads tampered with by hack...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] John Deere settles right-to-repair lawsuit for...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] South Cariboo firefighters contain bla...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Elevated pollution prompts air quali...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Film Club presents ‘H ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Chlorine Gas incident at Victoria po...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] VIDEO: Aldergrove zoo mourns passing...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] What’s in store for Canada’s 2026 wildfire seaso...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Energy storage breakthrough traps sunlight in a ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] A Mercury Rover Could Explore the Planet by Stick...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Dark Matter Experiment Reaches Critical Miles...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Brewster Kahle Is a Creative Force
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Stop blaming your 3D printer for slow print...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] 7 weird things you can do with a 3D printer th...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Scientists Solve 40-Year-Old Mystery of Sleep...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] The Bad Seed and the Problem of Blame
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] RapidRAW is the free, open-source Lightroom...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. unemployment rate spikes 0.6 percentage points to 6.7 per cent in March and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. government easing carbon tax for pulp mills as industry grapples with future, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What constitutes community resilience infrastructure in rural Cariboo communities, and who actually maintains it? Topics include: Fire response infrastructure and volunteer emergency services, Air quality monitoring and vulnerable population access to health information, Cultural institutions as psychological resilience resources, Community foundation endowment models for sustaining local infrastructure, Indigenous Guardian programs as models for sustained locally-rooted capacity.
